steps.title: Analyze the signs in region A
steps.content:
Region A contains points where $x < 0$ and $y > 0$. Let's examine what this means for the left side of the inequality:
  • Since $x < 0$, we have $2x < 0$
  • Since $y > 0$, we have $-6y < 0$
When both terms are negative, their sum is negative: $2x - 6y < 0$ for all points in region A.

steps.title: Compare to the inequality requirement
steps.content: The inequality requires $2x - 6y > 12$. But we showed that in region A, $2x - 6y < 0$. Since $0 < 12$, we have $2x - 6y < 0 < 12$ for every point in region A—the inequality can never be satisfied. That matches choice A — A is correct.
